title: Themes
meta_description: Build your own Astro themes for Static Website Generator. Clone the Theme Starter, customize it and package your theme for distribution.
og_description: Create custom Astro themes for Static Website Generator using the official Theme Starter.
excerpt: Build, customize and package your own Astro themes for Static Website Generator.
content:
# Themes

Static Website Generator themes are standard Astro projects. There is no proprietary templating language or custom build system to learn. If you know Astro, you already know how to build an SWG theme.

## Start with the Theme Starter

The easiest way to begin is by cloning the official Theme Starter repository.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/staticwebsitegenerator/theme-starter.git
cd swg-theme
npm install
npm run dev
```

Within minutes you'll have a fully working development environment containing example pages, blog articles, documentation and JSON content.

## Customize Everything

The Theme Starter is intended as a foundation. Replace the layouts, components and styling with your own design while keeping the required SWG templates in place.

Typical project structure:

```text
src/
├── components/
├── layouts/
├── templates/
├── lib/
└── styles/
```

Everything else is completely up to you.

## Build Using Astro

Themes are developed like any other Astro project.

```bash
npm run dev
```

Build a production version with:

```bash
npm run build
```

## Validate Your Theme

Before publishing your work, validate it against the SWG theme specification.

```bash
npm run validate
```

The validator checks the required templates, layouts and configuration files to ensure your theme is compatible with Static Website Generator.

## Package for Distribution

When your theme is ready, package it into an installable archive.

```bash
npm run package
```

The generated package can be imported directly into Static Website Generator using the Theme Manager.

## One Theme, Unlimited Websites

Because SWG separates content from presentation, a single theme can be reused across multiple websites. Simply import your theme, publish your content and build your site.

Whether you're creating a company website, documentation portal, blog or landing page, the same theme can power them all.
